title: alopecurus aequalis subsp. aristulatus
intro: Alopecurus aequalis subsp. aristulatus, also known as the great foxtail, is a type of grass that belongs to the Poaceae family. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating rheumatic pain, and acting as an expectorant to relieve respiratory issues. Alopecurus aequalis subsp. aristulatus can be prepared as an infusion or tincture for medicinal use, although its preparation and dosage should be done under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
